functional_requirements.title: Add Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can add an expense record with amount, category, description, and date. Each record gets a unique ID. Category must be one of: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other. Amount must be positive. Date defaults to today.

functional_requirements.title: List Expenses
functional_requirements.description: User can list all expense records with optional filtering by category and date range (start_date, end_date). Output shows ID, amount, category, description, date.

functional_requirements.title: Update Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can modify an expense record by ID. Fields that can be updated: amount, category, description. Must check if record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Delete Expense
functional_requirements.description: User can delete an expense record by ID. Must check if record exists.

functional_requirements.title: Get Summary
functional_requirements.description: User can get expense summary statistics grouped by category or date range. Returns total amount and amounts per category.

non_functional_requirements.description: All operations must provide clear success/failure messages.
Error handling for invalid IDs, negative amounts, invalid categories, and date range errors.

acceptance_criteria.description: All 5 operations (add, list, update, delete, summary) execute correctly.
Filtering by category and date range works correctly.
Error handling for invalid IDs, negative amounts, invalid categories.

storage.details: Data stored in memory during session, no persistence.

glossary.expense record: A single entry containing amount, category, description, date, and unique ID.
glossary.category: One of: food, transport, entertainment, shopping, other.

global_state_sources.description: Counter for generating unique expense IDs.

input_spec.description: Input is a JSON object with command and expense_data fields, passed via track_expense function.

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
